commit.message:
feat(routing): add startup validation + tests for LLM model routing

Prevents model drift by:
1. Validating at startup that all models in _AC_AGENT_ROUTING are
   supported by their provider's models list (logs error if not)
2. Adding unit tests that verify:
   - All configured models exist in provider configs
   - Code agents use codex model
   - Reasoning agents use gpt-5.2

These tests will fail in CI if someone adds an agent to _AC_AGENT_ROUTING
with a model that the LLM client doesn't support.

commit.message:
feat: Editor text decorations, language server support, and IDE demo (#220)

* feat: Add text decoration API for editor syntax highlighting

Implement Phase 1 of language server support — a low-level text decoration
API that enables per-character styling (foreground, background, bold, italic)
in the editor widget.

New types:
- TextDecoration: describes visual styling with theme-aware color resolution
- TextDecorationSpan: maps document position ranges to decorations with priority
- ITextDecorationProvider: interface for supplying decorations per render frame
- IEditorSession: handle for providers to interact with the editor
- SyntaxTheme: theme elements for common semantic token types (keyword, string,
  comment, number, type, function, etc.)

Editor integration:
- EditorWidget.Decorations(provider) fluent API for attaching providers
- TextEditorViewRenderer collects decorations from providers, resolves per-line
  decorations via BuildLineDecorations(), and applies them in RenderLine()
- Cursor/selection styling takes priority over decorations
- Multiple overlapping decorations resolved by priority per attribute

Demo & tests:
- LanguageServerDemo sample with TabPanel and C# syntax highlighting tab
- CSharpSyntaxHighlighter: static syntax highlighter using ITextDecorationProvider
- 8 tests covering fg/bg colors, cursor override, priority resolution,
  theme elements, multi-line spans, and combined attributes

Key fix: ResolvedDecoration array must be initialized with Hex1bColor.Default
(not default(Hex1bColor) which is RGB(0,0,0) with IsDefault=false).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Add underline decoration rendering with capability fallback

- Add UnderlineStyle and UnderlineColor to ResolvedDecoration struct
- Update BuildLineDecorations to resolve underline styles with priority
- Update RenderLine to emit underline ANSI codes (SGR 4:x, SGR 58)
- Implement fallback: curly/dotted/dashed → single when terminal lacks
  styled underline support; skip underline color when unsupported
- Add UnderlineColorThemeElement to TextDecoration for theme-aware
  underline colors
- Add DiagnosticTheme with error/warning/info/hint underline colors
- Add SupportsStyledUnderlines and SupportsUnderlineColor to
  TerminalCapabilities
- Create DiagnosticHighlighter demo provider with pattern-based
  error/warning/info/hint underlines
- Add Diagnostics tab to LanguageServerDemo sample
- Write 7 underline tests covering all styles, fallback, and theme
  resolution

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Fix underline rendering, color shift bug, and add line numbers

- Fix ConsolePresentationAdapter missing SupportsStyledUnderlines and
  SupportsUnderlineColor capabilities, which caused underlines to not
  render in real terminal sessions
- Rewrite RenderLine decoration state management to track active
  decoration state explicitly, properly resetting SGR attributes before
  cell type transitions (Normal→Cursor, Cursor→Normal) to prevent
  color shifts when cursoring through decorated text
- Add LineNumbers() fluent API to EditorWidget with line number gutter
  rendering in EditorNode, including gutter width calculation, viewport
  adjustment, and HitTest offset correction
- Add EditorTheme.LineNumberForegroundColor theme support
- Enable line numbers in LanguageServerDemo for both tabs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Add underline style and color support to Surface rendering pipeline

The Surface pipeline (SurfaceCell → SurfaceRenderContext → SurfaceComparer)
was stripping underline data — the ANSI sequences emitted by RenderLine
were parsed into cells that had no underline fields, causing all underline
information to be lost before reaching the terminal.

- Add UnderlineStyle and UnderlineColor fields to SurfaceCell
- Add colon sub-parameter parsing to SurfaceRenderContext.ParseSgrSequence
  for SGR 4:x (curly/dotted/dashed underlines)
- Add SGR 58 (underline color) parsing in both semicolon and colon syntax
- Add SGR 21 (double underline) and SGR 59 (default underline color) support
- Pass underline state when creating SurfaceCell instances
- Update SurfaceComparer.BuildSgrParameters to emit styled underline codes
  (4:3, 4:4, 4:5) and underline color (58;2;R;G;B) in output
- Track underline style/color state in the comparer diff loop

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: Enable mouse support in LanguageServerDemo

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Add editor overlay system with push/dismiss and hover demo

- EditorOverlay, OverlayLine, OverlayPlacement types for anchored UI
- IEditorSession expanded with PushOverlay/DismissOverlay/ActiveOverlays
- EditorNode implements IEditorSession with overlay lifecycle management
- Activate/Deactivate lifecycle for ITextDecorationProvider
- Overlay rendering with box-drawing borders and coordinate mapping
- DismissOnCursorMove support for hover-style overlays
- HoverInfoProvider demo showing diagnostic hover on cursor position
- Hover Info tab in LanguageServerDemo
- 9 overlay tests covering push/dismiss/lifecycle/rendering

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Add Language Server Protocol integration

Phase 4 implementation — complete LSP client with in-process test server:

Protocol layer:
- JsonRpcTransport: Content-Length framed JSON-RPC 2.0 over streams
  with concurrent read dispatching (request TCS + notification events)
- LspTypes: Position, Range, SemanticTokens, Diagnostics, Completion
- LspMessages: JSON-RPC request/response/notification types

Client:
- LanguageServerClient: lifecycle management (stdio/socket/transport),
  initialize handshake, document sync, semantic tokens, completions
- LanguageServerDecorationProvider: ITextDecorationProvider that bridges
  LSP semantic tokens + diagnostics to decoration spans
- SemanticTokenMapper: delta-encoded LSP tokens → TextDecorationSpans
- DiagnosticMapper: LSP diagnostics → underline decorations by severity

Public API:
- .LanguageServer(config => ...) extension on EditorWidget
- .WithServerCommand(), .WithSocket(), .WithTransport() for connections
- .WithLanguageId(), .EnableSemanticHighlighting(), .EnableDiagnostics()

Testing:
- TestLanguageServer: in-process LSP server over System.IO.Pipelines
- 7 integration tests: init, semantic tokens, diagnostics, completions
- Tests verify full pipeline: server → transport → client → mappers

Demo:
- InProcessLanguageServer for the LanguageServerDemo sample
- 'Language Server' tab showing LSP-powered syntax + diagnostics

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Fix LSP highlighting not appearing in demo

Three root causes:
1. LanguageServer() extension created a new provider every render frame,
   killing the LSP connection before it could return tokens. Fixed by
   caching providers via ConditionalWeakTable keyed by delegate identity.
2. IEditorSession.Invalidate() was a no-op — async token responses never
   triggered re-render. Now calls MarkDirty().
3. Demo used .LanguageServer() inline. Changed to pre-create provider
   outside the builder for stable instance identity.

Also made LanguageServerDecorationProvider public so consumers can create
and manage provider lifecycle explicitly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Add Hex1bLanguageServerWorkspace for shared server connections

LSP servers bind to a workspace root and handle multiple documents over
a single connection. The new workspace concept models this:

- Hex1bLanguageServerWorkspace: manages one LanguageServerClient per
  registered language, shared across all documents of that language
- RegisterServer(languageId, config): registers server configuration
  (lazy — server starts when first document opens)
- GetProvider(documentUri, languageId?): returns a cached decoration
  provider for a document, backed by the shared server connection
- Language ID inference from file extension (.cs→csharp, .py→python, etc.)

API usage:
  var workspace = new Hex1bLanguageServerWorkspace("/my/project");
  workspace.RegisterServer("csharp", lsp => lsp.WithServerCommand("csharp-ls"));
  ctx.Editor(state).LanguageServer(workspace, "file:///my/project/File.cs")

Also refactored:
- LanguageServerClient: multi-document (URI per method, version tracking
  per document, close all on shutdown)
- LanguageServerDecorationProvider: supports shared client via internal
  constructor, filters diagnostics by document URI
- Demo: Language Server tab shows two editors sharing one server

4 workspace tests + 7 LSP integration tests, all passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Fix LSP highlighting race condition with client readiness

Both workspace classes (Hex1bLanguageServerWorkspace, Hex1bDocumentWorkspace)
start the LanguageServerClient in a fire-and-forget Task.Run(). The provider
would then try to call OpenDocumentAsync() before StartAsync() completed,
hitting _transport == null and silently failing.

Fix:
- Add TaskCompletionSource-based WaitUntilReadyAsync() to LanguageServerClient
- Provider's OpenAndRefreshAsync() and OnDocumentChangedAsync() now await
  client readiness before sending any requests
- Extract token legend after readiness (workspace may not have had time
  to populate the legend when the provider was first created)

* Fix fold+scroll lockup, LSP re-render, and single-line fold filtering

- Add AppInvalidate callback on Hex1bNode base class so nodes can wake
  the render loop for async updates (LSP notifications). Set by Hex1bApp
  during reconciliation. EditorNode.Invalidate() now calls both
  MarkDirty() and AppInvalidate, ensuring diagnostics appear immediately.

- Filter out single-line folding regions in FoldingRangesToRegions()
  where endLine <= startLine after 0->1-based conversion, preventing
  useless fold indicators in the gutter.

- Clamp _horizontalScrollOffset and _scrollOffset in ArrangeCore after
  viewport dimension changes from folding or scrollbar toggling. Reset
  horizontal scroll when scrollbar is hidden.

- Make scrollbar click handlers only call MarkDirty() when offset
  actually changes, preventing unnecessary re-render storms.

- Robustify CalculateHorizontalThumb and CalculateVerticalThumb against
  division by zero and scroll fractions outside [0,1].

* Fix Ctrl+Space not reaching editor completion handler

Ctrl+Space sends a NUL byte (0x00) in traditional terminals, but
ParseKeyInput in both Hex1bTerminal and Hex1bAppWorkloadAdapter
did not handle the NUL case — it fell through to null and was
silently dropped.

Add '\0' → Hex1bKey.Spacebar with Hex1bModifiers.Control in both
ParseKeyInput implementations.

Also add CSI u (fixterm/Kitty protocol) support to ParseCsiSequence
so that modern terminals sending ESC[32;5u for Ctrl+Space are
handled correctly. The new ParseFixtermKeycode method maps Unicode
codepoints to Hex1bKey values for common keys.

Add benchmarks directory with scenario-based benchmark suite and fast ChannelIterator (#172)

* Initial plan

* Add benchmarks directory with full benchmark suite

- Framework: BenchConfig (target-tier iteration counts), BenchRunner
  (warmup + measure), BenchResult typedef, import.hx, Macro.hx
- Cases (cases/ subdirectory): BenchBasic, BenchTask, BenchChannel,
  BenchMutex, BenchGenerator
- BenchTask covers 7 task-architecture aspects: sequential_await,
  concurrent_latch, lazy_await, scope, supervisor, timeout_no_expire,
  nested (2-level hierarchy)
- Main.hx: formatted table output, per-target JSON persistence in
  results/latest-<target>.json (gitignored), regression comparison on
  re-run with 10% threshold
- Build files for eval, js, cpp, jvm, hl, python, neko, php
- .gitignore: benchmarks/bin/ and benchmarks/results/

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace micro-benchmarks with scenario-based benchmarks

Per feedback: the previous micro-benchmarks were too granular and
susceptible to GC/JIT jitter (notably on JVM). Replace them with five
application-style scenarios that each run for 0.2–1.5 s, providing a
much higher signal-to-noise ratio.

New cases (benchmarks/src/cases/):
- BenchWorkerPool  – WORKERS coroutines drain a bounded channel
- BenchPipeline    – 3-stage Source → Transform → Sink pipeline
- BenchContendedMutex – WORKERS compete for a mutex; yield() while
  holding forces genuine suspend/resume contention on every acquire
- BenchCancellation – repeated waves of spawning+cancelChildren() in a
  supervisor scope, measuring full cancellation teardown throughput
- BenchFanOut – scatter/gather: spawn WORKERS concurrent tasks (each
  does yield() + computation), then await and aggregate results

BenchGenerator kept unchanged (SelfDispatcher subsystem, already stable).

BenchConfig: replace SMALL/MEDIUM/LARGE with WORKERS/MESSAGES/ROUNDS/
GENERATOR_ITERS; 4-tier structure (cpp/jvm/hl, js, python/php, others).

BenchRunner: 5 warmup rounds for JVM (was 1) to reach JIT steady state.

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* introduce a performance regression

* Merge result and comparison tables into one

Remove the Iter column and the separate comparison block. printTable()
now accepts an optional previous:Null<Array<BenchResult>> and appends a
Change column inline when a baseline exists. Removes printComparison.

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add ChannelIterator: fast channel drain iterator

Replace the AsyncGenerator-based ChannelReaderTools.iterator() with a
dedicated ChannelIterator<T> that avoids per-item coroutine overhead.

The @:coroutine hasNext() method:
- Fast path: calls tryRead() first; when the buffer has items this
  succeeds immediately with no suspension at all
- Slow path: only calls waitForRead() when the buffer is empty,
  suspending once per "burst" of items rather than once per item
- Handles spurious wakeups with a while(true) retry loop

This restores pipeline/three_stage from ~2,000 to ~20,000 ops/sec on
eval (10x), matching the throughput of a manual waitForRead/tryRead
drain loop, while keeping the ergonomic `for (item in channel)` syntax.

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

- printTable() now accumulates elapsedMs across all rows and prints a
  separator + "total" row at the bottom of the table (both plain and
  Change-column layouts align correctly because the separator is reused)
- benchmarks/build-lua.hxml added, mirroring tests/build-lua.hxml
- lua added to getTarget() so results are saved as latest-lua.json

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* Tune BenchConfig: split HL tier, scale up interpreter targets

All targets now run for roughly 3-8 s total (previously ranged from
110 ms to 26 s):

- HL split from cpp/jvm into its own tier (25k/3k/100k) — HL channel
  ops are ~30x slower than CPP/JVM so the combined tier caused 26 s runs
- JS scaled up 2.5x (500k/30k/1M) — old config finished in ~1.6 s
- python/php split and each scaled up ~50x/~25x respectively (old

- eval/neko/lua scaled up ~1.6x (13k/5k/80k) — old config was ~3.8 s
- cpp/jvm unchanged (~7.6 s / ~4.8 s)

Verified totals: eval 6.9 s, python 7.1 s, php 5.4 s, hl 4.4 s.

mechanisms, while skillpm remains focused on portable Agent Skills
packaging and orchestration.

`add-mcp` for MCP config)

- Kept language aligned across repository README and docs homepage so
users get the same answer regardless of entry point.

- Plugin specs define host-specific extension/runtime hooks.
- Agent Skills define portable instruction packages that can be reused across agent systems.

For skillpm, the model stays the same: npm for dependency management, plus `skills` and `add-mcp` for wiring and MCP configuration.

feat: OpenClaw-style architecture with gateway, channels, memory, skills

Major refactor to follow OpenClaw patterns:
- WebSocket gateway server (ws://localhost:18789) as control plane
- Pluggable channel adapters: HTTP REST API, file inbox, CLI stdin
- SOUL.md agent identity system
- Persistent markdown-based memory with keyword search
- Pluggable skill registry (skills/ directory with manifest + handler)
- Composable system prompt builder (identity + memory + skills + session)
- Session management with SQLite-backed history
- Agent runtime with EventEmitter lifecycle events
- Full HTTP API: POST /api/tasks, GET /api/tasks/:id, GET /api/status

commit.message:
Fix 15 signal quality bugs, late-entry issues, and stat calculation errors (#84)

* Initial plan

* Fix 15 bugs, optimizations and add 19 new tests per deep code review

BUG 1: Add vol_threshold param to detect_market_structure_shift()
  - CH1=0.70 (default), CH2=0.60, CH3=0.50 - fixes gate blocker killing 50%+ signals
BUG 2: Add MACD/BB/CVD/EMA ribbon scoring to run_confluence_check_relaxed()
  - CH2 can now reach HIGH confidence (+40 possible extra points)
BUG 3: Normalize confluence score display to /100 in format_message()
  - Raw score ÷ 150 × 100, capped at 100 - accurate display
BUG 4: Asymmetric entry zone (LONG: low=price−ATR×0.5, high=price+ATR×0.15)
  - SHORT: low=price−ATR×0.15, high=price+ATR×0.5 - biased toward discount/premium
BUG 5: Stale-close deferred when current price is within entry zone
  - Prevents confusing STALE alerts while subscribers hold active positions
BUG 6: BE trigger uses entry_low (LONG) or entry_high (SHORT) as reference
  - Not midpoint - more accurate BE trigger for actual fill prices
BUG 7: SL hit after BE triggered now records as 'BE' outcome, not 'LOSS'
  - Prevents win-rate inflation from counting TP1+BE trades as WIN+LOSS
BUG 8: ATR floor for trailing SL (min_trail_distance_atr_mult=1.0)
  - Prevents noise-driven stop-outs in volatile markets
TIMING 1: Reduce AUTO_SCAN_INTERVAL_SECONDS default from 60 → 30
  - Halves signal latency for the REST fallback scanner
TIMING 2: WATCHING pre-alert via on_near_miss callback in run_confluence_check()
  - Broadcasts preparation alert when 1/5 gates fail (near-miss)
OPT 1: Extract _ema_series() to module level
  - Eliminates 2 duplicate nested function definitions
OPT 2: Incremental RSI in detect_rsi_divergence() (O(n) vs O(n²))
  - Uses Wilder's smoothing - ~10x faster for 20-candle windows
OPT 4: Signal ID line added to format_message() broadcast
CALC 2: Sharpe Ratio uses daily bucketing + sqrt(365) annualisation
  - Correct for 24/7 crypto markets (not 252 trading days)
Tests: 19 new tests covering all fixes

Co-authored-by: kishore446 <78694720+kishore446@users.noreply.github.com>
